content.text:
Recommendation Systems
Semester:
3  |
Type:
UEM3  |
VHS:
63h  |
Weekly hours:
C: 1h30 / TD: 1h30 / TP: 1h30  |
Personal work VHS:
42h  |
Coefficient:
2  |
Credits:
5
Teaching Objectives
Understand the fundamental principles of recommendation systems
Master the different recommendation approaches (collaborative, content-based, hybrid)
Develop recommendation systems
Evaluate the quality and relevance of recommendations
Know the challenges in this domain
Recommended prior knowledge:
Algorithmics, data structures, databases, machine learning, and programming in Python/Java.
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Introduction to Recommendation Systems
Definition and importance of recommendation systems
Applications in different domains (e-commerce, social media, etc.)
Evaluation of recommendation systems: precision, recall, F1-score, etc.
Collaborative Recommendation
User-based methods (similarity, neighbourhood)
Item-based methods (similarity, neighbourhood)
Algorithms: k-NN, Pearson, etc.
Advantages and disadvantages of collaborative recommendation
Content-Based Recommendation
Representation of items and users
Methods: content similarity, feature vectors
Algorithms: TF-IDF, word embeddings, etc.
Advantages and disadvantages of content-based recommendation
Hybrid Recommendation Systems
Combination approaches (weighting, concatenation, etc.)
Advantages and disadvantages of hybrid systems
Examples of hybrid systems (Netflix, Amazon, etc.)
Development and Evaluation of Recommendation Systems
Choice of tools and technologies (frameworks, libraries)
Implementation of recommendation algorithms
Performance evaluation and parameter tuning
Production deployment and monitoring
Challenges of Recommendation Systems
Data sparsity
New challenges (scalability, diversity, etc.)
Context-aware recommendation, personalisation
Content, service, and event recommendation
Assessment
In-person semester exam (60%)
Continuous assessment (CC) (40%): continuous tests, mini-project
References
Francesco Ricci, Lior Rokach, Bracha Shapira, Paul Kantor (2015).
Recommender systems handbook
. 2nd Edition, Springer.
Gediminas Adomavicius and Alexander Tuzhilin (2005). Toward the next generation of recommender systems.
IEEE Transactions on Knowledge & Data Engineering
, (6):734–749.
Yilmaz Ar and Erkan Bostanci (2016). A genetic algorithm solution to the collaborative filtering problem.
Expert Systems with Applications
, 61:122–128.
